title: StoryGuard Requirements Output Schema
description: Structured, implementation-ready requirements document for AI coding agent consumption. Every field is required unless marked optional. Vague or ambiguous values are not acceptable — if information is unavailable, mark the field as INFERRED or MISSING with a brief explanation.

metadata.description: Administrative context for this requirements document

intake_text.description: The original raw intake text verbatim — never modified

domain.description: Business domain inferred from intake context

user_story.description: The core user story in standard format

as_a.description: The specific user persona. Never use 'user' — must be a named role e.g. 'financial advisor', 'compliance officer', 'client'. If unknown, mark INFERRED and explain.

i_want.description: The specific capability or outcome requested. Must be actionable and unambiguous. No vague language like 'better', 'improve', 'handle'. If intake is vague, translate to a specific capability.

so_that.description: The business outcome or value delivered. Must be measurable or observable.

business_value.description: Relative business value based on domain context and stated urgency

priority_signal.description: Any urgency, compliance deadline, or stakeholder signal in the intake that implies priority

personas.description: All user types who will interact with or be affected by this story

role.description: Named role e.g. 'Financial Advisor', 'Compliance Officer', 'End Client', 'System Admin'

interaction_type.description: How this persona interacts with the feature

primary.description: True for the main persona the story is written for

needs.description: What this persona specifically needs from this feature

functional_requirements.description: Explicit, testable functional behaviors the system must exhibit. Each requirement must be independently verifiable.

requirement.description: Specific system behavior. Must start with 'The system shall' or 'The system must'. No vague language.

source.description: Whether this came directly from intake, was inferred from context, or is standard practice for this domain

testable.description: Can this requirement be verified with a specific test case?

non_functional_requirements.description: Quality attributes the system must exhibit. In financial services, these are compliance requirements — not optional enhancements.

requirement.description: Specific threshold e.g. 'Page load under 2 seconds for 95th percentile'. If MISSING, explain what should be defined.
Authentication, authorization, data encryption, session management requirements
Regulatory requirements — SEC, FINRA, SOC2, GDPR, state-level financial regulations as applicable

regulations_flagged.description: Specific regulations this story may touch

requirement.description: Uptime, error handling, failover, data integrity requirements
What actions must be logged, by whom, for how long, and who can access logs. Critical for financial services.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable Given/When/Then criteria. Every acceptance criterion must be binary — it either passes or fails. No subjective language.

when.description: The action or event that triggers the behavior

then.description: The expected, observable, measurable outcome. Must be binary — pass or fail.

test_type.description: The category of test this criterion covers

negative_test.description: Optional — the corresponding failure scenario that should NOT happen

system_dependencies.description: All systems this story touches upstream or downstream. Missing dependencies are a primary cause of sprint blockage.

upstream.description: Systems that provide data or services this story depends on

risk.description: What breaks if this dependency is unavailable or changes

downstream.description: Systems or processes that will be affected by changes in this story

impact.description: How this story's changes affect the downstream system

external_integrations.description: Third-party APIs, custodians, data feeds, or vendor systems

data_sources.description: Databases, data warehouses, or data feeds this story reads from or writes to

pii_involved.description: Does this data source contain personally identifiable information?

governance.description: AI governance layer — transparency about what was inferred, what was assumed, and what risks exist

performed.description: Whether PII check was run on intake text (via local Ollama)

flagged_terms.description: Any terms flagged as potentially sensitive

assumptions.description: Every inference made by the AI that was not explicitly stated in the intake. The BA must validate each one.

confidence.description: How confident the model is in this assumption based on domain context

validation_needed.description: What the BA should verify with the business stakeholder

hallucination_flags.description: Any specific claims in this document that may not be supported by the intake and should be verified before the story enters a sprint

field.description: Which field/section contains the potential hallucination

concern.description: What specifically may not be accurate

missing_information.description: Information that is required for a complete story but was not available in the intake or knowledge base

question_for_stakeholder.description: The specific question the BA should ask to fill this gap

human_review_required.description: Always true — no AI output enters a sprint without BA approval

quality_score.description: Completeness and quality assessment of this requirements document

dimension_scores.description: Score breakdown by dimension (each out of 2)

ready_for_sprint.description: True only if total_score >= 8.0 AND no hallucination_flags AND pii_check is clean

improvement_suggestions.description: Specific actions the BA can take to raise the score before sprint entry
